finding: Immutable repository and owner identity in an OIDC subject is necessary but insufficient. A deployment trust decision must also bind issuer, intended audience, exact workflow references, workflow commit, and token freshness in one deny-by-default evaluation.

verification.method: isolated Node test run using byte-identical pre-commit files

evidence_strength: executed deterministic local tests plus committed source
uncertainty: The evaluator validates decoded claims only. Signature verification, JWKS trust, nonce handling, and provider-side audience enforcement remain separate mandatory controls.
falsification_route: Reject or revise the policy if a retained, signature-verified GitHub OIDC token from the intended workflow uses materially different documented claim semantics, or if clock-skew measurements show the default tolerance is unsafe.
